Stablecoins and tokenization are driving the future of finance, and it's happening on Solana.

With our friends @Mastercard, @WesternUnion, and @Worldpay_Global, and 20+ infra providers, we're debuting SDP, a tokenization & payments orchestration platform to launch stablecoins, RWAs, and any permissioned asset on Solana.

You can now launch breakthrough digital finance products in weeks instead of months or years.

SDP is AI and API-first: point Codex or Claude Code at SDP and it will know what to do.

We'll be demoing this on stage today at Solana Accelerate Fintech with our lead product manager @catgu_ and @GuiBibeau, and I'll be at @blockworksDAS tomorrow as well to share more.

This has been a labor of love and we're delighted to debut this powerful new sandbox today.

SWQoS skips gossip and lands txs direct to the slot leader. Parsed transactions decode raw Solana output instantly. REST APIs cover balances, history, and metadata out of the box.

Free to start with a 2-day free trial. No credit card. Just build.

The focus is clear: every layer of execution and data, for builders and traders who refuse to slow down.

pekan kemaren gua kasih gratis agent gua ke hampir 100 orang buat test. agent yang sama yang hasilin gua 20$ per hari atau lebih.

gua open lagi buat 50 orang di komen.

$189.7 dalam 11 hari aktif. satu merah di tanggal 18, sisanya hijau semua. minggu ke-4 yang paling kenceng, $101.95 dalam satu hari di tanggal 22.

dalam hampir 2 minggu, agent gua kebanyakan lakuin 3 setup:

bid-ask tight di new pair yang volume-nya lagi tinggi. masuk cepet, keluar cepet, ambil fee dari volatilitas awal.

bid-ask 45-50 bins di token yang udah koreksi. ini yang paling sering. token udah turun, volume masih ada, range pas buat nangkap rebound sambil collect fees.

bid-ask default di token yang volume-nya masih jalan tapi belum koreksi banyak. lebih konservatif, hold lebih lama, tapi tetap profitable.

agent ini jalan 24 jam nonstop. gua cuma define cara entry dan exit biar range-nya efisien, sisanya dia handle sendiri.

sejauh ini masih profitable dan masih evolve.

@MeteoraAG @MeteoraIDN

data.translation:
Last week I gave away my agent for free to almost 100 people for testing. The same agent that generates me $20 per day or more.

I'm opening it up again for 50 people in the comments.

$189.7 in 11 active days. One red on the 18th, the rest all green. The 4th week was the strongest, $101.95 in one day on the 22nd.

A lot of people jumped right in to build, adding their own features on top of the framework I provided. Some added memory, some built a dashboard, some upgraded the screening pipeline. There were also some who just lurked, and that's fine.

In almost 2 weeks, my agent mostly does 3 setups:

Tight bid-ask on new pairs with high volume right now. Enter fast, exit fast, grab fees from the initial volatility.

Bid-ask 45-50 bins on tokens that have already corrected. This is the most common. Token's already down, volume's still there, range is just right to catch a rebound while collecting fees.

Default bid-ask on tokens where volume's still going but hasn't corrected much. More conservative, hold longer, but still profitable.

This agent runs 24 hours nonstop. I just define the entry and exit methods so the range is efficient, the rest it handles itself.

So far it's still profitable and still evolving.

@MeteoraAG @MeteoraIDN

data.translation:
before I could build meridian, the agent that generates 20$+ per day from lp on @MeteoraAG , I started from 0 coding skill. this is everything I built before getting there:

- PnL card generator @MeteoraIDN - learning to parse transactions on the blockchain
- Tools to help me with a ton of stuff, like tracker, pool analyzer
- Web dashboard investment tracker - learning to store and pull data from explorer
- Website MeteoraIDN (tools + multi-level user) - mastering Meteora SDK and combining it with DB
- Bismillah Dawo app - build and sign DLMM transactions directly, pull a bunch of data
- AI calorie tracker - mastering how to implement AI into real apps
- Scrapper Cookin/Fabriq - understanding how web requests work
- Meridian - combining everything I learned

on that journey I discovered OpenClaw and studied how it works in detail. what blew my mind the most was how they store every interaction and reinject it into the next prompt. 

btw hermes from @NousResearch > openclaw

that's what ultimately became the foundation of the memory system in Meridian, every closed position writes a lesson, that lesson gets filtered and injected into the next cycle, from there Meridian can evolve on its own.

oh yeah, I built all this using Claude. started with Codex, but switched to Claude when I began Meridian and the results were way better.

if anyone's asking if subbing to claude is worth it, totally worth it.

you don't need to be good at coding to start. build first, the learning follows on its own.

data.translation:
Every morning, my agent - Meridian - sends me a morning briefing. Yesterday, it opened 36 positions, closed 36, net PnL +$81.76, fees $127.93, win rate 64%. All of that with a cost of around $0.5 for the API key.

At 3 PM, I dropped a Telegram link to discuss the project.

But the most interesting part isn't the numbers. It's the "Lessons Learned" section.

In there, it explains what it learned from yesterday's positions and what it'll apply for the next deployment.

Yesterday, it noticed that in the current market conditions, ranges under 50 bins tend to underperform. From that, it decided on its own to prefer 50 bins and up for bid-ask in volatile markets.

Yeah, even though it rebels sometimes.

I didn't teach it that. It read it itself from the data of failed and successful positions, filtered the lessons, and injected them into the next decisions.

That's what amazes me about Meridian. It's not a machine that runs one task continuously without thinking. It evaluates, learns, and decides what's most sensible for that day's market conditions.

Every day, it's a little smarter than the day before.

1% better every day.

@MeteoraAG @MeteoraIDN

data.translation:
before I could build meridian, the agent that generates 20$+ per day from lp on @MeteoraAG , I started from 0 coding skill. this is everything I built before getting there:

- PnL card generator @MeteoraIDN - learning to parse transactions on the blockchain
- Tools to help me with a ton of stuff, like tracker, pool analyzer
- Web dashboard investment tracker - learning to store and pull data from explorer
- Website MeteoraIDN (tools + multi-level user) - mastering Meteora SDK and combining it with DB
- Bismillah Dawo app - build and sign DLMM transactions directly, pull a bunch of data
- AI calorie tracker - mastering how to implement AI into real apps
- Scrapper Cookin/Fabriq - understanding how web requests work
- Meridian - combining everything I learned

on that journey I discovered OpenClaw and studied how it works in detail. what blew my mind the most was how they store every interaction and reinject it into the next prompt. 

btw hermes from @NousResearch > openclaw

that's what ultimately became the foundation of the memory system in Meridian, every closed position writes a lesson, that lesson gets filtered and injected into the next cycle, from there Meridian can evolve on its own.

oh yeah, I built all this using Claude. started with Codex, but switched to Claude when I began Meridian and the results were way better.

if anyone's asking if subbing to claude is worth it, totally worth it.

you don't need to be good at coding to start. build first, the learning follows on its own.

result.text:
sebelum gua bisa build meridian, agent yang hasilin 20$+ per hari dari lp di @MeteoraAG , gua mulai dari 0 skill coding. ini semua yang gua build sebelum sampe sana:

- PnL card generator @MeteoraIDN - belajar parsing transaksi di blockchain
- Tools buat bantu gua dlmm banyak banget, kek tracker, pool analyzer
- Web dashboard investment tracker - belajar store dan pull data dari explorer
- Website MeteoraIDN (tools + multi-level user) - mahamin Meteora SDK dan combine sama DB
- Bismillah Dawo app - build dan sign transaksi DLMM langsung, pull banyak data
- AI calorie tracker - mahamin cara implementasi AI ke aplikasi nyata
- Scrapper Cookin/Fabriq - ngerti gimana request di web itu kerja
- Meridian -combine semua yang gua pelajarin

di perjalanan itu gua nemuin OpenClaw dan pelajarin cara kerjanya secara detail. yang paling bikin gua tercerahkan adalah gimana mereka store setiap interaksi dan inject ulang ke dalam prompt berikutnya. 

btw hermes dari @NousResearch > openclaw

itu yang akhirnya jadi fondasi memory system di Meridian, tiap posisi yang ditutup nulis lesson, lesson itu difilter dan di-inject ke siklus berikutnya, dari situ Meridian bisa evolve sendiri.

oh ya, gua build semua ini pake Claude. awalnya Codex, tapi switch ke Claude waktu mulai Meridian dan hasilnya jauh lebih gut.

kalo ada yang tanya subs claude worth ga, worth banget.

lu ga perlu jago coding buat mulai. build dulu, belajarnya ngikut sendiri.

tweets.full_text:
together-sol, 42 seconds, $52, 10.66%. not my usual play this month but when volume hits 500k per 5 minutes you don't just sit there.

bonk narrative was strong at the start. that's already a green flag before i even look at the chart. 

i played this strat 3 times, clean 10% each exit, before the TVL started exploding and the pool got too crowded to make it worth it.

setup was spot tight, 30 bins, single side sol only. tight because the volume was violent enough to fill bins fast and i wanted to be in and out before the momentum faded.

the timing window on this kind of play is short. once TVL balloons up the fee per bin drops and your edge disappears. i got in early, caught the volume, and stopped touching it when the pool got thick.

multi-days is still my main strat this month. but this is a good reminder that sometimes the market just hands you a setup that's too clean to ignore.

@MeteoraAG @met_lparmy
